etymology.description: 糸(糸) (sī, “silk; thread”) + 糸(糸) (sī, “silk; thread”) = two threads -> silk
etymology.interpretation: The character 絲(絲) (sī, "silk; thread") represents the concept of silk or thread through the duplication of the 糸(糸) (sī, "silk; thread") radical, emphasizing the intertwining and abundant nature of silk threads.
etymology.simplification: 絲 was simplified to 𢇁 by combining two instances of 糸(糸) into a single component that was later simplified to 丝, maintaining the resemblance to thread while reducing strokes.

examples.chinese: 这条裙子是用最好的丝制作的(這條裙子是用最好的絲製作的)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo qúnzi shì yòng zuì hǎo de sī zhìzuò de.
examples.english: This skirt is made of the finest silk.

examples.chinese: 她的头发像丝一样柔顺(她的頭髮像絲一樣柔順)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de tóufà xiàng sī yīyàng róushùn.
examples.english: Her hair is as smooth as silk.

examples.chinese: 那幅画上有几根细细的丝线(那幅畫上有幾根細細的絲線)。
examples.pinyin: Nà fú huà shàng yǒu jǐ gēn xìxì de sīxiàn.
examples.english: There are a few fine threads on that painting.
